scripts_found.description: Main PDF→Gemini→JSON→Firestore pipeline. Generates ms.*, derived.*, classification_v2, portfolio_exposure_v2.asset_mix (0-1 scale). Uses Gemini 2.5 Flash.
scripts_found.write_gate: Pipeline routing: only OK status writes. REVIEW writes require --write-review flag.
scripts_found.manual_protection: PRESERVES manual.costs.retrocession. Only initializes to 0 on new docs or when missing.
scripts_found.dry_run: NO --dry-run flag. Always writes if routing=OK.
scripts_found.backup: YES - saves raw_text, raw_llm, parsed_ms, canonical JSON locally before writing
scripts_found.snapshot: YES - reads existing doc before write to preserve retrocession

scripts_found.description: Original parser. Predecessor of cargador_lotes_v_2.js. Similar pipeline but lacks V2 classification, math validation, fixed-income hardening, and many of the V2 normalizations.
scripts_found.manual_protection: Similar pattern - does NOT overwrite manual

scripts_found.description: Python script that reads existing ms.* data from Firestore and generates classification_v2 + portfolio_exposure_v2 with economic_exposure (0-100 scale). Does NOT call Gemini. Does NOT parse PDFs.
scripts_found.write_gate: --execute flag required. Default mode is --dry-run (read-only preview).
scripts_found.manual_protection: PRESERVES all fields - uses update(), not set()
scripts_found.dry_run: YES - default mode is dry-run, --execute required for writes
scripts_found.backup: Saves batch report JSON to reports/ directory

scripts_found.description: Recalculates only derived.* fields from existing ms.* data. Does NOT touch manual, ms, or classification_v2.
Updates only ms.rating_stars from CSV export. DRY-RUN by default.
Legacy backfill for asset_class field. Superseded by V2 taxonomy.
Aggressive version of asset_class backfill. Superseded.

scale_analysis.asset_mix_normalization: Detects 0-100 via max_component > 1.0, divides by 100, rebases to sum=1.0
scale_analysis.economic_exposure_normalization: Detects 0-1 via total <= 1.05, multiplies by 100, rebases to sum=100
scale_analysis.risk_of_double_conversion: LOW - each source handles its own scale detection independently
scale_analysis.risk_of_ambiguous_zone: MEDIUM - values between 1.0-1.5 would NOT be divided by 100 by _as_fraction()
scale_analysis.explanation_for_220_funds_without_asset_: These 220 funds were processed ONLY by populate_taxonomy_v2.py (which generates economic_exposure at 0-100) and were NOT subsequently parsed by cargador_lotes_v_2.js (which generates asset_mix at 0-1). The parser only runs when PDFs are available.

gold_mining_natural_resources.parser_behavior: cargador_lotes_v_2.js correctly classifies as 'Commodities' via hardCommodities list. Maps to classification_v2.asset_type='alternative'. Gemini reports asset_allocation with equity=0, other=100.
gold_mining_natural_resources.populate_taxonomy_v2_behavior: Also classifies as COMMODITIES via text matching. Sets economic_exposure.other=100, equity=0.
gold_mining_natural_resources.root_cause_of_equity_0: Morningstar PDFs for gold/mining funds report asset_allocation.equity=0, asset_allocation.other=100 because the fund holds commodity-linked instruments, not direct equities. The parser faithfully reproduces Morningstar's categorization.
gold_mining_natural_resources.optimizer_impact: These 16 funds contribute 0 equity weight. If bucket bounds require minimum equity, these funds cannot fill that slot despite investing in mining company stocks.
gold_mining_natural_resources.recommendation: MANUAL OVERRIDE needed per-fund if the intent is to treat them as equity exposure.

retrocession_analysis.parser_behavior: cargador_lotes_v_2.js ONLY initializes manual.costs.retrocession=0 for NEW documents or when the field is completely missing. It NEVER overwrites existing retrocession values.
retrocession_analysis.gemini_prompt_exact_line: - NO extraigas: TER, retrocesiones, comisión de entrada o salida.

traceability_analysis.pdf_filename: YES - stored in manifest and canonical JSON
traceability_analysis.gemini_model: YES - stored in parser_version string and canonical payload
traceability_analysis.warnings: YES - comprehensive warning system in quality, classification_v2, portfolio_exposure_v2
traceability_analysis.raw_evidence: YES - raw_text, raw_llm, parsed_ms saved to local work directories
missing: no dry_run manifest (no dry-run mode in V2 parser)

cost_efficiency.batch_support: YES - p-limit concurrency with configurable concurrency
cost_efficiency.retry_support: YES - up to 6 retries with exponential backoff
cost_efficiency.reparsing_avoidance: PARTIAL - PDFs are moved to processed/review/error dirs after processing, but no ISIN-level dedup check
cost_efficiency.csv_cost_reduction: N/A - CSV is used only for subcategory mapping, not for reducing Gemini calls

220_funds_without_asset_mix.cause: Processed by populate_taxonomy_v2.py only (generates economic_exposure 0-100). Never passed through cargador_lotes_v_2.js (generates asset_mix 0-1).
220_funds_without_asset_mix.severity: MEDIUM - runtime fallback handles this via get_v2_asset_mix()

16_equity_with_0_percent_equity.cause: Morningstar PDF reports equity=0, other=100 for gold/mining funds. Parser faithfully reproduces this.
16_equity_with_0_percent_equity.severity: HIGH - semantic gap affects optimizer bucket bounds

11_derived_stale.cause: derived.asset_class uses Spanish labels (Inmobiliario, Monetario, Otros) while classification_v2 uses V2 enum (REAL_ESTATE, MONETARY, EQUITY). Different systems, not synced.
11_derived_stale.severity: LOW - optimizer uses classification_v2, not derived

retrocessions_out_of_range.cause: NOT caused by parser. Parser never writes retrocessions. These values were loaded by separate manual/batch import processes.

risks.detail: cargador_lotes_v_2.js and cargador_lotes.js REQUIRE serviceAccountKey.json. This file was removed during OPT-5/OPT-7 security cleanup. The parser cannot run without it.
risks.recommendation: Migrate to ADC-only authentication like populate_taxonomy_v2.py and recalculate_derived_fields.js already do.

risks.detail: cargador_lotes_v_2.js has NO --dry-run flag. When run, it ALWAYS writes to Firestore for OK-routed funds. The only gate is the routing status.
risks.recommendation: Add --dry-run flag that skips Firestore writes but still produces canonical JSONs.

risks.detail: 16 gold/mining/natural resources funds get equity=0% because Morningstar classifies the underlying as commodity, not equity. This distorts optimizer bucket bounds.
risks.recommendation: Add post-parser override rules for known commodity-equity patterns, or create manual classification overrides.

risks.detail: cargador_lotes_v_2.js generates asset_mix at 0-1. populate_taxonomy_v2.py generates economic_exposure at 0-100. Both are valid but create confusion in audits.
risks.recommendation: Document this as intentional design. Consider future unification to single scale.

risks.detail: cargador_lotes.js (V1) and backfill scripts are superseded but still present. Running them accidentally would cause data regression.
risks.recommendation: Archive or delete legacy scripts, or add deprecation guards.

risks.detail: Parser does not log who initiated the run. No operator/user field in manifests.
risks.recommendation: Add --operator flag to record who ran the batch.

risks.detail: If recalculate_derived_fields.js is run after cargador_lotes_v_2.js, it may overwrite derived.* with slightly different logic (less comprehensive token matching).
risks.recommendation: Standardize derived computation to single source.

risks.detail: Parser (production core infrastructure, 106KB) is mixed with 52 heterogeneous maintenance scripts in scripts/maintenance/. No structural distinction between core pipeline and one-shot utilities.

3151 lines, 66 functions, 0 module.exports. Impossible to unit test sanitizeAssetMixForExposureBuilder() or deriveAssetClass() without executing the entire file (requires Gemini key + serviceAccountKey).
risks.recommendation: Extract pure functions to lib/*.js modules with module.exports.

risks.detail: recalculate_derived_fields.js and refresh_derived_data.js reimplement parser functions via copy-paste with subtle divergences.
risks.recommendation: Refactor to import from shared morningstar_parser/lib/ modules.

risks.detail: Parser expects subcategory_sectors_mapping.csv and subcategory_tokens_mapping.csv in __dirname (scripts/maintenance/) but they only exist in data/work/ and functions_python/scripts/. Parser would fail on startup.
risks.recommendation: Move CSVs to morningstar_parser/config/ or update path references.

risks.detail: scripts/package.json mixes parser deps (pdf-parse, @google/generative-ai, csv-parse) with unrelated deps (xlsx, minimist).
New developer cannot distinguish production pipeline from one-shot diagnostic script by looking at the directory. Parser has no README.

config_init.concern: CLI args, paths, Gemini init, Firebase init, CSV loading

helpers.concern: File I/O, hashing, string cleaning, date parsing

normalization.concern: Asset allocation, regions, sectors, fixed income, market cap, style box

gemini_extraction.concern: Prompt template, extraerMSConGemini, JSON response parsing

pdf_processing.concern: processPdfFile orchestrator, builds canonical payload, writes Firestore

proposed_structure.prerequisite: ADC migration (R1-HIGH) must happen before or simultaneously
